# Varity

> Build, deploy, and monetize apps with one command. Auth, database, storage, and payments included.

## What is Varity

Varity is a developer platform that provides open-source packages and a CLI for building, deploying, and monetizing production applications. Install two packages, run one command, and your app is live with auth, storage, database, and hosting already configured.

- Target audience: Web developers (React, Next.js, TypeScript)
- No blockchain knowledge required — infrastructure complexity is fully abstracted
- 70% cheaper than AWS/GCP/Azure
- Revenue split: 90% to developer, 10% to Varity platform

### How It Works

1. Developer installs `@varity-labs/sdk` and `@varity-labs/ui-kit`
2. Developer builds their app using familiar React/Next.js patterns
3. Developer runs `varitykit app deploy` — app is live with auth, database, and hosting
4. Developer submits to Varity App Store — users pay with credit cards
5. Developer earns 90% of revenue

### Cost Comparison

| Scenario | AWS | Varity | Savings |
|----------|-----|--------|---------|
| 100 users + AI | $2,800/mo | ~$800/mo | ~70% |

---

## Packages

### @varity-labs/sdk (v2.0.0-beta.2)

Core SDK for database, credentials, and app configuration.

Install: `npm install @varity-labs/sdk`

#### Database API

```typescript
import { db } from '@varity-labs/sdk';

// Typed collection
interface User {
  name: string;
  email: string;
  role: 'admin' | 'member' | 'viewer';
  createdAt: string;
}

const users = db.collection<User>('users');

// Create — returns the full document with id at top level
const user = await users.add({
  name: 'Alice',
  email: 'alice@example.com',
  role: 'admin',
  createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
});
console.log(user.id);    // 'doc_abc123' (auto-generated)
console.log(user.name);  // 'Alice' (flat, NOT user.data.name)

// Read all
const allUsers = await users.get();
console.log(allUsers.length);

// Read with pagination and sorting
const page2 = await users.get({ limit: 10, offset: 10 });
const newest = await users.get({ orderBy: '-createdAt' });
const sorted = await users.get({ limit: 20, orderBy: 'name' });

// Client-side filtering (NO .where() method)
const admins = allUsers.filter(u => u.role === 'admin');

// Update — returns updated document
const updated = await users.update('doc_abc123', { name: 'Alice Smith' });
console.log(updated.name); // 'Alice Smith'

// Delete — returns boolean
const success = await users.delete('doc_abc123');
console.log(success); // true
```

#### QueryOptions

```typescript
interface QueryOptions {
  limit?: number;      // Maximum number of documents to return
  offset?: number;     // Number of documents to skip (pagination)
  orderBy?: string;    // Field to sort by ("field" ascending, "-field" descending)
}
```

#### Document Type

```typescript
interface Document {
  id: string;
  created_at?: string;
  updated_at?: string;
  [key: string]: any;
}
```

Documents from `.add()`, `.get()`, `.update()` are `T & Document` — all fields are flat. Access `doc.name`, NOT `doc.data.name`.

#### Custom Database Configuration

```typescript
import { Database } from '@varity-labs/sdk';

const customDb = new Database({
  proxyUrl: 'https://custom-db.example.com',
  appToken: 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9...',
});

const products = await customDb.collection('products').get();
```

#### Credentials API

```typescript
import {
  resolveCredentials,        // Auto-resolve from env vars with dev fallback
  isUsingDevCredentials,     // Check if using shared dev creds
  isProductionCredentials,   // Check if using production creds
  getCredentialWarning,      // Get warning message for dev creds
  logCredentialUsage,        // Log credential info (deduplicated)
  validateCredentials,       // Validate credential format
  getUpgradeInstructions,    // Instructions for upgrading to prod creds
  VARITY_DEV_CREDENTIALS,   // Shared dev credentials object
} from '@varity-labs/sdk';

// Auto-resolve credentials
const creds = resolveCredentials(
  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_APP_ID,
  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_THIRDWEB_CLIENT_ID
);

// Check environment
if (isUsingDevCredentials(creds.privy.appId)) {
  const warning = getCredentialWarning('production');
  if (warning) console.warn(warning);
}
```

#### Version

```typescript
import { VERSION, SDK_VERSION } from '@varity-labs/sdk';
console.log(VERSION); // '2.0.0-beta.2'
```

#### Advanced Subpath Imports

```typescript
// Platform configuration
import { varityL3Testnet, formatUSDC, parseUSDC, USDC_DECIMALS } from '@varity-labs/sdk/chains';
console.log(varityL3Testnet.id); // 33529
const amount = parseUSDC('99.50'); // 99500000n
const formatted = formatUSDC(99500000n); // "99.50"

// Infrastructure utilities (advanced)
import { BlockchainService, NFTLicensingService, RevenueSplitService } from '@varity-labs/sdk/blockchain';

// Thirdweb helpers (advanced)
import { createThirdwebWrapper, StorageClient, BridgeClient } from '@varity-labs/sdk/thirdweb';

// Event tracking (advanced)
import { createGasTracker, calculateGasInUSDC } from '@varity-labs/sdk/tracking';
```

#### Auto-Configuration

The `db` singleton auto-resolves credentials from environment variables:
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_DB_PROXY_URL` or `VITE_VARITY_DB_PROXY_URL` or `REACT_APP_VARITY_DB_PROXY_URL`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_APP_TOKEN` or `VITE_VARITY_APP_TOKEN` or `REACT_APP_VARITY_APP_TOKEN`
- Fallback: Development credentials (automatic)

---

### @varity-labs/ui-kit (v2.0.0-beta.3)

React component library for auth, dashboards, analytics, forms, and UI primitives.

Install: `npm install @varity-labs/ui-kit`

All imports: `import { ComponentName } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';`

#### Providers

**PrivyStack** — All-in-one auth provider. Wrap your app root.

```tsx
<PrivyStack
  appId="your-privy-app-id"
  loginMethods={['email', 'google', 'wallet']}
  appearance={{ theme: 'light', accentColor: '#2563EB', logo: '/logo.png' }}
>
  {children}
</PrivyStack>
```

Props: `appId` (string), `thirdwebClientId` (string), `loginMethods` (string[], default: ['email','google','wallet']), `appearance` ({theme, accentColor, logo}), `onAddressChange` ((address: string|null) => void), `children` (ReactNode, required).

**VarityDashboardProvider** — Full-featured dashboard provider with auth, React Query, wallet sync.

```tsx
<VarityDashboardProvider
  privyAppId={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_APP_ID}
  appearance={{ theme: 'light', logo: '/logo.png' }}
>
  <Dashboard />
</VarityDashboardProvider>
```

Props: `privyAppId` (string), `thirdwebClientId` (string), `appearance` ({theme, accentColor, logo}), `loginMethods` (string[]), `children` (ReactNode, required).

**ThemeProvider** — CSS variable-based theming.

```tsx
import { ThemeProvider, defaultTheme, themePresets } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

<ThemeProvider theme={{ ...defaultTheme, primaryColor: '#6a1b9a' }}>
  <App />
</ThemeProvider>

// Or use presets: themePresets.finance, .healthcare, .retail, .tech
```

Props: `theme` (VarityTheme, required), `children` (ReactNode, required).

**ToastProvider** — Toast notification context. Wrap app to enable `useToast()`.

```tsx
<ToastProvider>{children}</ToastProvider>

// In any child:
const toast = useToast();
toast.success('Saved!');
toast.error('Failed');
toast.info('Processing...');
```

#### Authentication Components

**PrivyLoginButton** — Pre-built login button that opens auth modal.

```tsx
<PrivyLoginButton />
<PrivyLoginButton onSuccess={() => router.push('/dashboard')} onError={console.error} className="btn-primary">
  Sign In to Continue
</PrivyLoginButton>
```

Props: `children` (ReactNode, default: "Sign In"), `onSuccess` (function), `onError` (function), `className` (string).

**PrivyUserProfile** — User info display with optional logout.

```tsx
<PrivyUserProfile showLogoutButton onLogout={() => router.push('/')} />
```

Props: `showLogoutButton` (boolean, default: false), `onLogout` (function), `className` (string).

**PrivyProtectedRoute** — Shows content only when authenticated.

```tsx
<PrivyProtectedRoute fallback={<CustomLoginPage />} loadingComponent={<Spinner />}>
  <Dashboard />
</PrivyProtectedRoute>
```

Props: `children` (ReactNode), `fallback` (ReactNode, default: login prompt), `loadingComponent` (ReactNode, default: spinner).

**PrivyReadyGate** — Prevents blank screen during auth init (5-15 seconds).

```tsx
<PrivyReadyGate timeout={10000} initializingScreen={<Splash />} timeoutScreen={<Error />}>
  {children}
</PrivyReadyGate>
```

Props: `timeout` (number, default: 10000), `initializingScreen` (ReactNode), `timeoutScreen` (ReactNode).

**Re-exported hooks:** `usePrivy`, `useWallets`, `useLogin`, `useLogout` from @privy-io/react-auth.

```tsx
const { authenticated, user, ready, logout } = usePrivy();
const email = user?.email?.address;
```

#### Payment Components (post-beta)

**PaymentWidget** — In-app payment for purchases and upgrades.

```tsx
<PaymentWidget
  appId={123}
  price={999}
  type="subscription"
  intervalDays={30}
  onSuccess={(txHash) => console.log('Paid!', txHash)}
  onError={(err) => console.error(err)}
  onCancel={() => console.log('Cancelled')}
  theme="light"
>
  <button>Subscribe for $9.99/mo</button>
</PaymentWidget>
```

Props: `appId` (number, required), `price` (number, cents), `type` ('one-time'|'subscription'), `intervalDays` (number, default: 30), `onSuccess` ((txHash: string) => void), `onCancel` (() => void), `onError` ((error: Error) => void), `theme` ('light'|'dark'), `className` (string), `children` (ReactNode, required), `disabled` (boolean).

**PaymentGate** — Content gate that shows premium content after purchase.

```tsx
<PaymentGate appId={123} price={999} fallback={<p>Purchase to unlock.</p>}>
  <PremiumContent />
</PaymentGate>
```

Props: `appId` (number, required), `price` (number), `fallback` (ReactNode, required), `children` (ReactNode, required), `theme` ('light'|'dark'), `className` (string).

**useVarityPayment** — Hook for programmatic payment.

```tsx
const { hasPurchased, purchase, isLoading, isPurchasing, pricing, error, refresh } = useVarityPayment({ appId: 123 });
```

#### Dashboard Components

**DashboardLayout** — Main layout with sidebar, header, footer, content area. NO mobile support — implement mobile nav manually.

```tsx
<DashboardLayout
  companyName="Acme Corp"
  logoUrl="/logo.png"
  user={{ name: 'Jane Doe', address: 'jane@acme.com' }}
  navigationItems={[
    { label: 'Dashboard', icon: 'dashboard', path: '/' },
    { label: 'Analytics', icon: 'analytics', path: '/analytics' },
    { label: 'Settings', icon: 'settings', path: '/settings' },
  ]}
  onNavigate={(path) => router.push(path)}
  onLogout={() => logout()}
  onSearchClick={() => setShowPalette(true)}
  showSidebar={true}
  showHeader={true}
  showFooter={true}
  sidebarWidth={240}
  headerHeight={64}
>
  <YourContent />
</DashboardLayout>
```

Props: `children` (required), `showSidebar` (boolean, true), `showHeader` (boolean, true), `showFooter` (boolean, true), `sidebarWidth` (number, 240), `headerHeight` (number, 64), `logoUrl` (string), `companyName` (string), `navigationItems` (NavigationItem[]), `user` (UserInfo), `onLogout` (() => void), `onNavigate` ((path: string) => void), `onSearchClick` (() => void), `searchPlaceholder` (string).

Types: `NavigationItem: { label, icon?, path, active?, children? }`, `UserInfo: { name, address, avatarUrl? }`.

**DashboardHeader** — Top nav bar with user menu, notifications, search.

Props: `height` (number, 64), `user` (UserInfo), `onLogout` (() => void), `showNotifications` (boolean, true), `notificationCount` (number, 0), `onSearchClick` (() => void), `searchPlaceholder` (string, 'Search...').

**DashboardSidebar** — Fixed sidebar with collapsible nav.

Props: `width` (number, 240), `logoUrl` (string), `companyName` (string, 'Dashboard'), `navigationItems` (NavigationItem[]), `onNavigate` ((path: string) => void), `collapsed` (boolean, false).

**DashboardFooter** — Footer with Varity attribution (required).

Props: `companyName` (string), `showAttribution` (boolean, true), `links` (FooterLink[]). Type: `FooterLink: { label, url, external? }`.

#### Analytics Components

**KPICard** — Key performance indicator with trend.

```tsx
<KPICard title="Revenue" value="$12,345" trend="up" trendValue="+12%" variant="default" size="md" />
```

Props: `title` (required), `value` (string|number, required), `subtitle` (string), `icon` (ReactNode), `trend` ('up'|'down'|'neutral'), `trendValue` (string), `loading` (boolean), `onClick` (() => void), `variant` ('default'|'outlined'|'filled'), `size` ('sm'|'md'|'lg').

**EnhancedKPICard** — Rich KPI with sparkline, trend badge, help tooltip, sync time.

```tsx
<EnhancedKPICard
  title="Monthly Revenue"
  value="$45,230"
  icon="money"
  trend="up"
  color="green"
  change={{ value: 12.5, period: 'vs last month' }}
  showSparkline
  sparklineData={[30, 35, 32, 40, 38, 45]}
  helpText="Total revenue from all sources"
  lastSynced="2 min ago"
/>
```

Props: `title` (required), `value` (required), `icon` (string, required), `trend` ('up'|'down'|'neutral'), `color` ('blue'|'green'|'orange'|'purple'|'red'), `change` ({value: number, period: string}), `showSparkline` (boolean), `sparklineData` (number[]), `source` (string), `helpText` (string), `lastSynced` (string), `onClick` (() => void).

**AnalyticsCard** — Metric card with mini chart.

Props: `title` (required), `value` (string|number, required), `unit` (string), `trend` ('up'|'down'|'neutral'), `trendValue` (number), `comparisonPeriod` (string), `icon` (string), `loading` (boolean), `chartData` (number[]), `onClick` (() => void).

**DataTable** — Sortable, paginated data table.

```tsx
<DataTable
  columns={[
    { key: 'name', header: 'Name', sortable: true },
    { key: 'revenue', header: 'Revenue', align: 'right', render: (val) => `$${val.toLocaleString()}` },
    { key: 'status', header: 'Status' },
  ]}
  data={[
    { id: '1', name: 'Product A', revenue: 12000, status: 'Active' },
    { id: '2', name: 'Product B', revenue: 8500, status: 'Draft' },
  ]}
  pagination
  pageSize={10}
  onRowClick={(row) => console.log(row)}
  hoverable
  striped
/>
```

Props: `columns` (DataTableColumn[], required), `data` (T[], required), `loading` (boolean), `pagination` (boolean), `pageSize` (number, 10), `onRowClick` ((row: T) => void), `emptyMessage` (string), `hoverable` (boolean, true), `striped` (boolean, true). Column type: `{ key, header, width?, sortable?, render?, align? }`.

**ChartContainer** — Chart wrapper with title, actions, loading/error/empty states.

Props: `title` (required), `subtitle` (string), `children` (required), `actions` (ChartAction[]), `height` (number|string, 400), `loading` (boolean), `error` (string), `isEmpty` (boolean), `emptyMessage` (string). Type: `ChartAction: { label, icon?, onClick }`.

**MetricDisplay** — Simple metric with formatting.

```tsx
<MetricDisplay label="Revenue" value={125000} format="currency" decimals={0} size="large" color="success" />
<MetricDisplay label="Conversion" value={3.5} format="percentage" />
```

Props: `label` (required), `value` (string|number, required), `format` ('number'|'currency'|'percentage'|'custom'), `currencySymbol` (string, '$'), `decimals` (number, 0), `size` ('small'|'medium'|'large'), `color` ('default'|'primary'|'success'|'warning'|'error'), `icon` (string).

**Sparkline** — SVG mini line chart.

Props: `data` (number[], required, min 2), `width` (number, 100), `height` (number, 32), `strokeColor` (string, '#3b82f6'), `fillColor` (string), `strokeWidth` (number, 2), `showGradient` (boolean, true). Color presets: `SPARKLINE_COLORS.positive`, `.negative`, `.neutral`, `.blue`, `.purple`, `.orange`. Helper: `getSparklineColors('up'|'down'|'neutral')`.

**StatusBadge** — Status indicator with colored dot.

Props: `status` (StatusType, required), `label` (string), `showDot` (boolean, true), `size` ('sm'|'md'|'lg'), `icon` (ReactNode). StatusType: 'connected'|'disconnected'|'pending'|'syncing'|'error'|'warning'|'expired'|'active'|'inactive'.

**IntegrationStatus** — Integration status with sync time.

Props: `isConnected` (boolean, required), `needsReauth` (boolean), `isSyncing` (boolean), `lastSyncTime` (Date).

**EmptyState** — Placeholder for empty views.

```tsx
<EmptyState
  title="No projects yet"
  description="Get started by creating your first project."
  action={{ label: 'Create Project', onClick: () => setShowModal(true) }}
/>

// Presets
<EmptyStatePresets.NoResults />
<EmptyStatePresets.NoData action={{ label: 'Add', onClick: handleAdd }} />
<EmptyStatePresets.NoIntegrations />
<EmptyStatePresets.ConnectionRequired />
<EmptyStatePresets.ComingSoon />
```

Props: `title` (required), `description` (string), `icon` (ReactNode), `action` ({label, onClick, variant?}), `secondaryAction` ({label, onClick}), `size` ('sm'|'md'|'lg').

**LoadingSkeleton** — Animated loading placeholders.

```tsx
<LoadingSkeleton type="card" />
<LoadingSkeleton type="table" items={5} />
<LoadingSkeleton type="text" lines={3} />

// Shorthand components
<SkeletonCard />
<SkeletonTable items={5} />
<SkeletonText lines={4} />
<SkeletonList items={3} />
```

Props: `type` ('text'|'circle'|'rect'|'card'|'table'|'list'), `width` (string|number), `height` (string|number), `lines` (number, 3), `items` (number, 3), `animate` (boolean, true).

#### Form Components

**Button** — Multi-variant button.

```tsx
<Button variant="primary" onClick={handleSave}>Save</Button>
<Button variant="secondary" size="sm">Cancel</Button>
<Button variant="danger" loading>Deleting...</Button>
<Button variant="outline" icon={<PlusIcon />}>Add Item</Button>
<Button variant="ghost" size="lg">Learn More</Button>
```

Props: `variant` ('primary'|'secondary'|'outline'|'ghost'|'danger', default: 'primary'), `size` ('sm'|'md'|'lg', default: 'md'), `loading` (boolean), `icon` (ReactNode), `disabled` (boolean). Also accepts all `<button>` HTML attributes.

**Input** — Text input with label, error, hint.

```tsx
<Input label="Email" type="email" placeholder="you@example.com" required error={errors.email} hint="We'll never share your email." />
```

Props: `label` (string), `error` (string), `hint` (string). Also accepts all `<input>` HTML attributes.

**Textarea** — Multi-line text input.

Props: `label` (string), `error` (string). Also accepts all `<textarea>` HTML attributes.

**Select** — Dropdown select.

```tsx
<Select
  label="Role"
  options={[
    { value: 'admin', label: 'Admin' },
    { value: 'member', label: 'Member' },
  ]}
  error={errors.role}
/>
```

Props: `label` (string), `options` ({value: string, label: string}[], required), `error` (string). Also accepts all `<select>` HTML attributes.

**Toggle** — Switch toggle.

```tsx
<Toggle checked={enabled} onChange={setEnabled} label="Email Notifications" description="Receive updates via email" />
```

Props: `checked` (boolean, required), `onChange` ((checked: boolean) => void, required), `label` (ReactNode), `description` (string), `disabled` (boolean), `size` ('sm'|'md').

**Checkbox** — Checkbox with label, description, indeterminate.

```tsx
<Checkbox checked={agreed} onChange={setAgreed} label="I agree to the terms" />
<Checkbox checked={false} indeterminate={true} onChange={handleChange} label="Select all" />
```

Props: `checked` (boolean, required), `onChange` ((checked: boolean) => void, required), `label` (string), `description` (string), `disabled` (boolean), `indeterminate` (boolean), `error` (string).

**RadioGroup** — Radio button group.

```tsx
<RadioGroup
  name="plan"
  label="Select Plan"
  value={selectedPlan}
  onChange={setSelectedPlan}
  options={[
    { value: 'free', label: 'Free', description: 'Basic features' },
    { value: 'pro', label: 'Pro', description: '$9/month' },
  ]}
  orientation="vertical"
/>
```

Props: `value` (string, required), `onChange` ((value: string) => void, required), `options` (RadioOption[], required), `name` (string, required), `label` (string), `orientation` ('vertical'|'horizontal', default: 'vertical'), `disabled` (boolean), `error` (string). Type: `RadioOption: { value, label, description?, disabled? }`.

#### Overlay Components

**Dialog** — Modal dialog with backdrop, focus trap, escape-to-close.

```tsx
<Dialog open={showDialog} onClose={() => setShowDialog(false)} title="Edit Profile" description="Update your info.">
  <form>
    <Input label="Name" />
    <Button onClick={handleSave}>Save</Button>
  </form>
</Dialog>
```

Props: `open` (boolean, required — NOT `isOpen`), `onClose` (() => void, required), `title` (string, required), `description` (string), `children` (ReactNode, required).

**ConfirmDialog** — Confirmation dialog with confirm/cancel.

```tsx
<ConfirmDialog
  open={showConfirm}
  onClose={() => setShowConfirm(false)}
  onConfirm={handleDelete}
  title="Delete Project"
  description="This action cannot be undone."
  confirmLabel="Delete"
  confirmVariant="danger"
  loading={isDeleting}
/>
```

Props: `open` (boolean, required), `onClose` (() => void, required), `onConfirm` (() => void, required), `title` (string, required), `description` (string, required), `confirmLabel` (string, 'Delete'), `confirmVariant` ('danger'|'primary', 'danger'), `loading` (boolean).

**DropdownMenu** — Accessible dropdown with keyboard nav.

```tsx
<DropdownMenu
  trigger={<Button variant="ghost">Actions</Button>}
  items={[
    { label: 'Edit', icon: EditIcon, onClick: handleEdit },
    { label: 'Duplicate', icon: CopyIcon, onClick: handleDuplicate },
    { type: 'separator' },
    { label: 'Delete', icon: TrashIcon, onClick: handleDelete, variant: 'danger' },
  ]}
/>
```

**CommandPalette** — Cmd+K command palette with search, keyboard nav, sections.

```tsx
<CommandPalette
  open={showPalette}
  onClose={() => setShowPalette(false)}
  onSelect={(item) => router.push(item.path)}
  items={[
    { id: '1', label: 'Dashboard', section: 'Navigation', path: '/dashboard' },
    { id: '2', label: 'Settings', section: 'Navigation', path: '/settings' },
    { id: '3', label: 'New Project', section: 'Actions', path: '/new' },
  ]}
  placeholder="Type a command or search..."
/>
```

---

### @varity-labs/types (v2.0.0-beta.2)

TypeScript type definitions for all Varity interfaces.

Install: `npm install @varity-labs/types`

Key types:

```typescript
import type {
  DatabaseConfig,      // { proxyUrl?: string; appToken?: string }
  QueryOptions,        // { limit?: number; offset?: number; orderBy?: string }
  Document,            // { id: string; created_at?: string; updated_at?: string }
  CollectionResponse,  // Response wrapper type
  CredentialConfig,    // { privy: { appId: string }; thirdweb: { clientId: string } }
} from '@varity-labs/types';
```

---

## CLI: varitykit (v1.1.4)

Install: `pip install varitykit`
Requires: Python 3.10+, Node.js 18+

### All Commands

**varitykit doctor** — Check environment and dependencies.
```bash
varitykit doctor
```

**varitykit init [name]** — Scaffold new project from template.
```bash
varitykit init my-app
varitykit init my-app --template saas-starter
```

**varitykit app deploy** — Deploy to production.
```bash
varitykit app deploy
varitykit app deploy --hosting static
varitykit app deploy --hosting dynamic
varitykit app deploy --submit-to-store
varitykit app deploy --name my-cool-app
varitykit app deploy --tier starter
varitykit app deploy --mode guided
```

Options:
- `--hosting` — `static` (default, CDN/IPFS) or `dynamic` (container hosting). Auto-detected.
- `--path` — Project directory (default: `.`)
- `--submit-to-store` — Submit to Varity App Store after deploy
- `--tier` — Pricing tier: `free`, `starter`, `growth`, `enterprise`, `scale`
- `--name` — Custom app name for `varity.app/{name}`
- `--mode` — Deployment mode: `auto` (default), `guided`, or `expert`

**varitykit app list** — List all deployments.
```bash
varitykit app list
```

**varitykit app info <id>** — Get deployment details.
```bash
varitykit app info deploy-1737492000
```

**varitykit app status** — View overall deployment status.
```bash
varitykit app status
```

**varitykit app rollback <id>** — Revert to a previous deployment.
```bash
varitykit app rollback deploy-1737491000
```

**varitykit platforms** — Show available deployment platforms.
```bash
varitykit platforms
```

### What Happens at Deploy Time

1. CLI analyzes source code for database/auth usage
2. If database detected: generates app_id + JWT token + db_proxy_url
3. Creates temporary `.env.local` with credentials (all framework prefixes)
4. Builds your app (env vars compiled in)
5. Uploads built app to hosting
6. Deletes `.env.local` (credentials embedded in build output)
7. If `--submit-to-store`: opens Developer Portal in browser

### Supported Frameworks

| Framework | Detection | Build Command |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| Next.js | `next.config.*` | `npm run build` |
| React (Vite) | `vite.config.*` | `npm run build` |
| React (CRA) | `react-scripts` | `npm run build` |
| Vue | `vue.config.*` | `npm run build` |
| Custom | `package.json` | `npm run build` |

---

## Templates

### SaaS Starter (default)

Create: `npx create-varity-app my-app`

Production-ready Next.js 15 template with:
- Authentication (email, Google, GitHub, Twitter, Discord)
- Dashboard with sidebar navigation
- Settings page (profile, preferences, security, billing)
- Landing page with animations
- Command palette (Cmd+K)
- 20+ UI components
- Database integration
- Projects, Tasks, Team CRUD pages

#### Template Structure

```
src/
  app/                    # Next.js pages
    page.tsx             # Landing page
    login/page.tsx       # Login page
    dashboard/
      layout.tsx         # Dashboard layout (sidebar + mobile nav)
      page.tsx           # Dashboard home
      projects/          # Projects CRUD
      tasks/             # Tasks CRUD
      team/              # Team management
      settings/          # Settings
  lib/
    varity.ts            # SDK import (export { db } from '@varity-labs/sdk')
    database.ts          # Collection helpers
    hooks.ts             # React hooks for data
    constants.ts         # App name, navigation items
  types/
    index.ts             # TypeScript interfaces
  components/ui/         # Shared UI components
```

#### Customization

**Change branding:**
1. `src/lib/constants.ts` — Change `APP_NAME`
2. `src/app/globals.css` — Change CSS variables (`:root { --color-primary-* }`)
3. `tailwind.config.js` — Colors reference CSS variables

**Add a new page:**
1. Create `src/app/dashboard/new-page/page.tsx`
2. Add to `NAVIGATION_ITEMS` in `src/lib/constants.ts`
3. Available icons: dashboard, folder, list, people, settings

**Add a new data model:**
1. Define interface in `src/types/index.ts`
2. Add collection in `src/lib/database.ts`: `export const items = () => db.collection<Item>('items')`
3. Create hook in `src/lib/hooks.ts` (copy `useProjects` pattern with optimistic updates)
4. Build page component using the hook

**Navigation items:**
```typescript
// src/lib/constants.ts
{ label: 'My Page', icon: 'star', path: '/dashboard/my-page' }
```

---

## Development Pattern (Complete)

### Type -> Collection -> Hook -> Page

#### Step 1: Define TypeScript interface

```typescript
// src/types/index.ts
export interface Customer {
  id?: string;          // Auto-generated UUID, always optional
  name: string;
  email: string;
  plan: 'free' | 'pro' | 'enterprise';
  createdAt: string;    // ISO string
}
```

Rules:
- `id` is always `string | undefined` — auto-generated UUID
- Use ISO strings for dates (`new Date().toISOString()`)
- Collection names: snake_case plural (`customers`, `order_items`)
- Keep interfaces flat — no nested objects

#### Step 2: Create collection accessor

```typescript
// src/lib/database.ts
import { db } from '@varity-labs/sdk';
import type { Customer } from '../types';

export const customers = () => db.collection<Customer>('customers');
```

#### Step 3: Build hook with optimistic updates

```typescript
// src/lib/hooks.ts
'use client';
import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';
import { customers } from './database';
import type { Customer } from '../types';

export function useCustomers() {
  const [data, setData] = useState<Customer[]>([]);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);

  const refresh = useCallback(async () => {
    try {
      setLoading(true);
      setError(null);
      const result = await customers().get();
      setData(result as Customer[]);
    } catch (err) {
      setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load');
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => { refresh(); }, [refresh]);

  const create = async (input: Omit<Customer, 'id' | 'createdAt'>) => {
    const optimistic = {
      ...input,
      id: `temp-${Date.now()}`,
      createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    };
    setData(prev => [optimistic, ...prev]); // Optimistic add
    try {
      await customers().add({ ...input, createdAt: optimistic.createdAt });
      await refresh(); // Sync with server
    } catch (err) {
      setData(prev => prev.filter(c => c.id !== optimistic.id)); // Rollback
      throw err;
    }
  };

  const update = async (id: string, changes: Partial<Customer>) => {
    const previous = data;
    setData(prev => prev.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...c, ...changes } : c)); // Optimistic
    try {
      await customers().update(id, changes);
      await refresh();
    } catch (err) {
      setData(previous); // Rollback
      throw err;
    }
  };

  const remove = async (id: string) => {
    const previous = data;
    setData(prev => prev.filter(c => c.id !== id)); // Optimistic
    try {
      await customers().delete(id);
    } catch (err) {
      setData(previous); // Rollback
      throw err;
    }
  };

  return { data, loading, error, create, update, remove, refresh };
}
```

#### Step 4: Use hook in page component

```tsx
// src/app/dashboard/customers/page.tsx
'use client';
import { useCustomers } from '@/lib/hooks';
import { DataTable, Button, EmptyState } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

export default function CustomersPage() {
  const { data, loading, error, create, remove } = useCustomers();

  if (loading) return <LoadingSkeleton type="table" />;
  if (error) return <p>Error: {error}</p>;
  if (data.length === 0) return <EmptyState title="No customers yet" action={{ label: 'Add Customer', onClick: handleAdd }} />;

  return (
    <DataTable
      columns={[
        { key: 'name', header: 'Name', sortable: true },
        { key: 'email', header: 'Email' },
        { key: 'plan', header: 'Plan' },
      ]}
      data={data}
      pagination
      pageSize={10}
    />
  );
}
```

---

## Auth Pattern (Complete)

```tsx
// app/layout.tsx — wrap entire app
import { PrivyStack } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <body>
        <PrivyStack appId={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_APP_ID}>
          {children}
        </PrivyStack>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

```tsx
// app/dashboard/layout.tsx — protect dashboard routes
import { PrivyProtectedRoute } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

export default function DashboardLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <PrivyProtectedRoute>
      {children}
    </PrivyProtectedRoute>
  );
}
```

```tsx
// Any component — access user info
'use client';
import { usePrivy } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

export function UserGreeting() {
  const { user, authenticated, logout, ready } = usePrivy();

  if (!ready) return <p>Loading...</p>;
  if (!authenticated) return <PrivyLoginButton />;

  const email = user?.email?.address;
  return (
    <div>
      <p>Welcome, {email}</p>
      <button onClick={logout}>Sign out</button>
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

## Deploy (Complete)

### Static Hosting (default)

```bash
npm run build
varitykit app deploy
# App is live at https://your-app.varity.app
```

Best for: marketing sites, SPAs, dashboards, landing pages.
Features: global CDN, free hosting, permanent URLs, automatic SSL.

### Dynamic Hosting

```bash
npm run build
varitykit app deploy --hosting dynamic
```

Best for: APIs, server-side rendering, full-stack apps.
Features: container hosting, persistent storage, 60-80% cheaper than AWS.
No Docker required on developer's machine — the CLI handles containerization.

### Deploy + App Store

```bash
npm run build
varitykit app deploy --submit-to-store
```

### Next.js Static Export

For static hosting, add to `next.config.js`:

```javascript
const nextConfig = {
  output: 'export',
  typescript: { ignoreBuildErrors: true },
};
module.exports = nextConfig;
```

No dynamic routes (`[id]`) with static export. Use client-side state instead.

---

## Environment Variables

### Required for Development

```
NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_APP_ID=         # Auth (optional — shared dev creds used if omitted)
NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_APP_ID=        # Your app ID (set after first deploy)
NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_APP_TOKEN=     # JWT for DB access (auto-generated at deploy)
NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_DB_PROXY_URL=  # DB proxy URL (auto-injected at deploy)
```

### Framework Prefixes

The SDK auto-resolves from multiple prefixes:
- Next.js: `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`
- Vite: `VITE_*`
- CRA: `REACT_APP_*`

### Credential Injection at Deploy Time

1. CLI generates: app_id, JWT token, db_proxy_url
2. Written to `.env.local` with all framework prefixes
3. Build compiles env vars into output
4. `.env.local` deleted after build
5. Credentials embedded in production build

---

## Monetization

### App Store Submission

```bash
varitykit app deploy --submit-to-store
```

Or submit after deploying via the MCP server:
```
varity_submit_to_store(deployment_id, name, description, price)
```

### Revenue Split

- 90% goes to the developer
- 10% goes to Varity platform
- Users pay with credit cards (no crypto required)

### Pricing Tiers

Set with `--tier` flag: `free`, `starter`, `growth`, `enterprise`, `scale`.

---

## MCP Server (@varity-labs/mcp v1.0.1)

The Varity MCP server gives AI editors 7 tools for the full build -> deploy -> monetize workflow. Runs locally via stdio. No API keys, no accounts.

### Setup

**Cursor** — Add to `.cursor/mcp.json`:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "varity": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@varity-labs/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

**Claude Code:**
```bash
claude mcp add varity -- npx -y @varity-labs/mcp
```

**VS Code (Copilot)** — Add to `.vscode/mcp.json`:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "varity": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@varity-labs/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

**Windsurf** — Add to `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json`:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "varity": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@varity-labs/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Tools Reference

**varity_search_docs** — Search Varity docs for guides, API references, tutorials. Read-only.
- `query` (string, required) — Search query
- `maxResults` (number, default: 3) — Max results

**varity_cost_calculator** — Compare Varity costs against AWS/Vercel. Read-only.
- `users` (number, required) — Monthly active users
- `storage_gb` (number, default: 10) — Storage in GB
- `has_database` (boolean, default: true)
- `has_auth` (boolean, default: true)

**varity_init** — Scaffold a new app. Creates files.
- `name` (string, required) — Project name (lowercase, hyphens)
- `template` ("saas-starter", default: "saas-starter")
- `path` (string, default: current directory)

**varity_deploy** — Build and deploy. Destructive.
- `path` (string, default: workspace root)
- `submit_to_store` (boolean, default: false)

**varity_deploy_status** — Check deployments. Read-only.
- `deployment_id` (string, optional — omit to list all)

**varity_deploy_logs** — Get build logs. Read-only.
- `deployment_id` (string, required)
- `limit` (number, default: 100)

**varity_submit_to_store** — Publish to App Store. Destructive.
- `deployment_id` (string, required)
- `name` (string, required) — Display name
- `description` (string, required) — Short description
- `price` (number, required) — Monthly price in USD (0 for free)

### Response Format

```typescript
// Success
{ success: true, data: { /* tool-specific */ }, message: "Human-readable summary" }

// Error
{ success: false, error: { code: "ERROR_CODE", message: "What went wrong", suggestion?: "How to fix" } }
```

### Typical Workflow

1. "Create a Varity app called my-saas" -> varity_init
2. "What will this cost for 200 users?" -> varity_cost_calculator
3. (Build features)
4. "Deploy this" -> varity_deploy
5. "Did the deploy succeed?" -> varity_deploy_status
6. "Show me the build logs" -> varity_deploy_logs
7. "Submit to the app store at $14.99" -> varity_submit_to_store

---

## Cursor Rules

AI-powered development assistance rules for Cursor IDE.

Install:
```bash
curl -fsSL https://docs.varity.so/cursor-rules/install-varity-rules.sh | bash
```

Available rules: `varity-deploy.mdc`, `varity-auth.mdc`, `varity-storage.mdc`, `varity-errors.mdc`, `varity-testing.mdc`, `varity-typescript.mdc`, `varity-performance.mdc`, `varity-contracts.mdc`.

---

## System Prompts

Downloadable prompts for AI assistants:
- App Development: https://docs.varity.so/prompts/varity-app-development.md
- Database Design: https://docs.varity.so/prompts/database-schema-design.md
- Deployment Guide: https://docs.varity.so/prompts/deployment-guide.md
- Template Customization: https://docs.varity.so/prompts/customize-template.md

---

## Key Rules for AI Assistants

### Terminology
- NEVER use blockchain/crypto terminology in user-facing code
- Use "deploy" not "upload to IPFS"
- Use "sign in" not "connect wallet"
- Use "usage fees are covered" not "gasless transactions"
- Use "platforms" not "chains"

### Code Patterns
- Use TypeScript interfaces for all data models
- Use optimistic UI updates (update state first, then API, rollback on error)
- Use `'use client'` directive for hooks and interactive components
- `.get()` returns all docs — filter on client side
- No `.where()` or `.find()` method — all filtering is client-side
- `id` field is always `string | undefined` — auto-generated UUID
- Collection names: snake_case plural (e.g., `customers`, `order_items`)
- Keep interfaces flat — no nested objects
- Use ISO strings for dates (`new Date().toISOString()`)

### Component Gotchas
- DashboardLayout has NO mobile support — implement mobile nav manually
- Dialog prop is `open` (NOT `isOpen`)
- Documents from `.add()`, `.get()`, `.update()` are flat — access `doc.name` NOT `doc.data.name`
- Database credentials are auto-injected at deploy time
- Static export: use `output: 'export'` in next.config.js
- No dynamic routes (`[id]`) with static export — use client-side state
- React 18/19 type conflict: fix with `typescript: { ignoreBuildErrors: true }` in next.config.js
- DashboardFooter `showAttribution` must be `true` (required by license)

### Database Limitations (Beta)
- No `.where()` filtering — filter client-side
- No real-time updates — use polling
- `.get()` returns ALL documents — use `{ limit, offset }` for pagination

---

## Troubleshooting

### Common Issues

**"Module not found"**
```bash
npm install @varity-labs/sdk @varity-labs/ui-kit @varity-labs/types
```

**"CLI not found"**
```bash
pip install varitykit
```

**Build errors with TypeScript**
```javascript
// next.config.js
{ typescript: { ignoreBuildErrors: true } }
```

**Static export with dynamic routes**
Use `output: 'export'` in next.config.js. Replace `[id]` routes with client-side state.

**Database not working in dev**
Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_APP_TOKEN` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_DB_PROXY_URL` in `.env.local`.

**MCP server "Cannot find module"**
Ensure Node.js 18+ is installed: `node --version`.

**MCP deploy tools "varitykit not found"**
Install CLI: `pip install varitykit`. Verify: `varitykit doctor`.

**MCP server not showing in Cursor**
Restart Cursor after adding config. Validate `.cursor/mcp.json` is valid JSON.

---
